unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */
/* buffer dimensioning: * use 16K as chunk size, as that fits H2 DATA frames well */
/* this is how much we want "in flight" for a stream */
/* on receiving from TLS, we prep for holding a full stream window */
/* on send into TLS, we just want to accumulate small frames */
/* stream recv/send chunks are a result of window / chunk sizes */
/* keep smaller stream upload buffer (default h2 window size) to have * our progress bars and "upload done" reporting closer to reality */
/* spare chunks we keep for a full window */

/* We need to accommodate the max number of streams with their window * sizes on the overall connection. Streams might become PAUSED which * will block their received QUOTA in the connection window. And if we * run out of space, the server is blocked from sending us any data. * See #10988 for an issue with this. */

function.name: populate_settings
populate_binsettings

unit.comment: /* How to access `call_data` from a cf_h2 filter */

function.name: cf_h2_ctx_clear
cf_h2_ctx_free

unit.function_decl: static CURLcode h2_progress_egress(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data);

function.name: drain_stream
http2_data_setup
free_push_headers
http2_data_done
h2_client_new
nw_in_reader
nw_out_writer

unit.function_decl: static int on_frame_recv(nghttp2_session *session, const nghttp2_frame *frame, void *userp);
static int on_frame_send(nghttp2_session *session, const nghttp2_frame *frame, void *userp);
static int on_stream_close(nghttp2_session *session, int32_t stream_id, uint32_t error_code, void *userp);
static int on_begin_headers(nghttp2_session *session, const nghttp2_frame *frame, void *userp);
static int error_callback(nghttp2_session *session, const char *msg, size_t len, void *userp);

function.name: cf_h2_ctx_init
should_close_session

unit.comment: /* * Processes pending input left in network input buffer. * This function returns 0 if it succeeds, or -1 and error code will * be assigned to *err. */

function.name: h2_process_pending_input

unit.comment: /* * The server may send us data at any point (e.g. PING frames). Therefore, * we cannot assume that an HTTP/2 socket is dead just because it is readable. * * Check the lower filters first and, if successful, peek at the socket * and distinguish between closed and data. */

function.name: http2_connisalive
http2_send_ping
Curl_http2_ver
nw_out_flush

unit.comment: /* * The implementation of nghttp2_send_callback type. Here we write |data| with * size |length| to the network and return the number of bytes actually * written. See the documentation of nghttp2_send_callback for the details. */

function.name: send_callback

unit.comment: /* We pass a pointer to this struct in the push callback, but the contents of the struct are hidden from the user. */

function.name: curl_pushheader_bynum
curl_pushheader_byname
h2_duphandle
set_transfer_url
discard_newhandle
push_promise
recvbuf_write_hds
on_stream_frame
fr_print
on_frame_send

unit.comment: /* !CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS */

function.name: on_frame_recv
on_data_chunk_recv
on_stream_close
on_begin_headers

unit.comment: /* frame->hd.type is either NGHTTP2_HEADERS or NGHTTP2_PUSH_PROMISE */

function.name: on_header
req_body_read_callback
function.parameter_list: (nghttp2_session *session, int32_t stream_id, uint8_t *buf, size_t length, uint32_t *data_flags, nghttp2_data_source *source, void *userp)

function.name: error_callback
Curl_http2_request_upgrade
http2_data_done_send
http2_handle_stream_close
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, struct h2_stream_ctx *stream, CURLcode *err)

function.name: sweight_wanted
sweight_in_effect

unit.comment: /* * h2_pri_spec() fills in the pri_spec struct, used by nghttp2 to send weight * and dependency to the peer. It also stores the updated values in the state * struct. */

function.name: h2_pri_spec

unit.comment: /* * Check if there's been an update in the priority / * dependency settings and if so it submits a PRIORITY frame with the updated * info. * Flush any out data pending in the network buffer. */

function.name: h2_progress_egress
stream_recv
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, struct h2_stream_ctx *stream, char *buf, size_t len, CURLcode *err)

function.name: h2_progress_ingress
cf_h2_recv
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, char *buf, size_t len, CURLcode *err)

function.name: h2_submit
cf_h2_send
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, const void *buf, size_t len, CURLcode *err)

function.name: cf_h2_adjust_pollset
cf_h2_connect
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, bool blocking, bool *done)

function.name: cf_h2_close
cf_h2_destroy
http2_data_pause
cf_h2_cntrl
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, int event, int arg1, void *arg2)

function.name: cf_h2_data_pending
cf_h2_is_alive
cf_h2_keep_alive
cf_h2_query
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, int query, int *pres1, void *pres2)

unit.decl_stmt: struct Curl_cftype Curl_cft_nghttp2 = { "HTTP/2", CF_TYPE_MULTIPLEX, CURL_LOG_LVL_NONE, cf_h2_destroy, cf_h2_connect, cf_h2_close, Curl_cf_def_get_host, cf_h2_adjust_pollset, cf_h2_data_pending, cf_h2_send, cf_h2_recv, cf_h2_cntrl, cf_h2_is_alive, cf_h2_keep_alive, cf_h2_query, };

function.name: http2_cfilter_add
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter **pcf, struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, int sockindex, bool via_h1_upgrade)

function.name: http2_cfilter_insert_after
Curl_cf_is_http2
Curl_conn_is_http2
Curl_http2_may_switch
Curl_http2_switch
Curl_http2_switch_at
Curl_http2_upgrade

unit.comment: /* Only call this function for a transfer that already got an HTTP/2 CURLE_HTTP2_STREAM error! */

function.name: Curl_h2_http_1_1_error

unit.comment: /* !USE_NGHTTP2 */
/* Satisfy external references even if http2 is not compiled in. */

function.name: curl_pushheader_bynum
curl_pushheader_byname

unit.comment: /* USE_NGHTTP2 */
